Description: Replace the shared ADMIN_TOKEN with proper admin login, ideally Microsoft 365, Azure AD, Google Workspace SSO, or another company identity provider.
Instead of maintaining allowed emails manually in .env, connect to a company user list, Azure AD, Google Directory, Supabase table, or HR export.
Extend the workflow with assignedTo, updatedAt, resolvedAt, and internal IT notes in addition to Open, In Progress, Resolved, and Closed.
Allow IT staff to assign tickets to specific technicians such as Mohammed, Hasan, or Jessica.
Keep a timeline of status changes, internal notes, replies, and who changed what.
Send IT an email when a ticket is created and notify employees when ticket status changes.
After submission, show a clean success screen with ticket ID, expected response time, and reference details.
Add an assignee column so admins can quickly see who owns each ticket.
Show when each ticket was last changed so stale tickets are easier to identify.
Add SLA or due-time information to help prioritize urgent or overdue tickets.
Add filters for department, assignee, date range, and unresolved tickets.
Allow sorting by priority, creation date, last updated date, and SLA risk.
Use a side panel instead of a popup if the dashboard grows and admins need faster navigation.
Add quick actions such as Assign to me, Mark resolved, and Add internal note.
Show tickets today, average resolution time, overdue tickets, and tickets by department.
Move fully to Supabase or another database instead of relying on data/tickets.json.
If using Supabase, configure access rules carefully and keep sensitive operations server-side.
Add logs for errors, ticket creation, ticket updates, and admin actions.
Validate all fields and add rate limiting to reduce spam or abuse.
Set up backups if ticket data is important for company operations.
Record who changed a ticket, what changed, and when it happened.
Keep .env secrets safe and avoid committing real tokens or service keys.
Replace free-text department with a controlled dropdown list.
Show additional questions depending on issue type, such as asset tag for hardware or location for network issues.
Allow users to upload screenshots or supporting files.
Add help text so users understand when to choose Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Use the verified email mapping to auto-fill the employee name.
Add bilingual support if company users need Arabic and English.
Add ticket assignment, internal notes, and updated timestamp next because it makes the dashboard much closer to a real IT helpdesk tool while staying simple.
